run_summary: Screen 1 evaluates 7 recent flags from two AI-event catalysts (Anthropic Fable 5 and Apple WWDC). Two positions (AMBA, TENB) entered yesterday remain intact after 1 day; both are held. Of the 7 new flags, a basket of 4 Fable-driven names (OMCL, CGNX, PLAB, HCAT) qualifies for entry, but the basket rule applies — all 4 convert to exploratory tier. AMBA and TENB re-flagged at lower confidence (3 each) and are already held; no new action. LRN and VRRM are exploratory-tier candidates from the Apple catalyst, also entered exploratory. Hard cap on exploratory positions (6 max per screen) means VRRM converts to WATCH to preserve dry powder.

entries.name: Omnicell, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: An Anthropic cybersecurity model has zero overlap with hospital pharmacy automation. Omnicell sells DEA-regulated physical dispensing cabinets and consumable blister packs that require a BAA-bound vendor and physical install — Fable doesn't touch that TAM. The curation false-victim pattern (physical robotics, Schedule II compliance) is corroborated by the filings.
entries.what_kills: An actual customer-concentration shift in the 10-Q, a missed earnings print, or a debt/covenant 8-K — the K already flags substantial debt and SaaS-renewal risk, so a fundamental miss would invalidate the sympathy-fade thesis.
entries.reasoning: Fable 5 catalyst applies to a basket of 4 names (OMCL, CGNX, PLAB, HCAT). OMCL passes: threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified', confidence=4. Filings show pharmacy robotics/DEA-regulated dispensing are orthogonal to a cybersecurity LLM. However, basket rule applies (4 names from same trigger): all convert to exploratory tier (6% of equity) rather than conviction, to avoid 60%+ concentration bet on a single Fable reading. Position represents a test of the sympathy-fade thesis across the set.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes a cybersecurity LLM release cannot cascade into healthcare IT procurement cycles, but hospital IT budgets and cyber-compliance purchasing are tightly coupled.
red_team.critique: While Fable is not a vision model and OMCL's physical dispensing cabinets are hardware-based, the thesis underestimates the downstream procurement signal. Hospitals typically bundle cyber-compliance, vendor BAA standards, and IT infrastructure upgrades in the same budget cycle. A major cybersecurity AI release can trigger IT audits of existing vendor portfolios, including Omnicell's cloud/SaaS dependencies. The 10-K flags 'SaaS-renewal risk' explicitly, which is exactly the vulnerability a cybersecurity LLM announcement touches. The 5-day decline of -8.86% is material enough to suggest the market is pricing a real (if transient) risk beyond pure sympathy.

entries.name: Cognex Corporation
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: Anthropic's Fable is a cybersecurity LLM — it has no plausible overlap with deterministic factory-floor machine vision. CGNX's moat is integrator networks, deterministic latency, and an installed base on production lines. The fade is purely thematic 'AI-tech selloff' contagion, compounded by an already-extended weekly drawdown.
entries.what_kills: The 10-K does acknowledge open-source/AI vision as a real future risk, so a credible competing release (e.g. a Google/NVIDIA vision platform aimed at industrial QA) within the window would shift this from minimal to indirect. Also watch for a guidance cut from large auto/EV customer slowdown.
entries.reasoning: Basket-rule trigger: Fable 5 catalyst, same cohort as OMCL/PLAB/HCAT. Passes BUY eligibility: threat_assessment='minimal' (10-K acknowledges open-source AI vision as future risk but Fable is not a vision model), panic_calibration='unjustified', confidence=4. 5-day move is -11.16%, suggesting extended tape already prices some repricing; exploratory tier fits the 'test position' framing without overcommitting. Deterministic factory-floor vision moat (integrator networks, latency) is untouched by cybersecurity LLM.

red_team.weakest_link: The moat (deterministic latency, integrator networks) is real, but the 5-day move of -11.16% already prices substantial repricing, leaving limited upside for an exploratory position.
red_team.critique: The thesis that Fable is irrelevant to factory-floor vision is correct in isolation, but the 5-day decline of -11.16% is extended relative to typical sympathy fades in this cohort. An exploratory position sizing (6% of equity) on a name already down double-digits over 5 days means the entry is chasing a partially-realized move, reducing the margin of safety. The 10-K's explicit acknowledgment of 'open-source AI vision as future risk' also suggests the market is not pure-thematic on Fable; some selling may be legitimate pre-emptive positioning against a broader AI-vision competitive threat, even if Fable itself is not that threat.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: Photronics is structurally a beneficiary of AI capex (more chips → more masks), and Fable is a cybersecurity model with no semiconductor or capex implication. The selloff is mechanically backwards: a cyber-model release does not reduce TSMC/Samsung mask demand. Curation hypothesis is well-supported.
entries.what_kills: A genuine semis-capex guidance cut from a major foundry, or a tariff/export-control headline that hits PLAB's China operations (a real risk per the K).
entries.reasoning: Basket-rule trigger: Fable 5 catalyst cohort with OMCL/CGNX/HCAT. Passes BUY eligibility: threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified', confidence=4. Filings show zero AI-competitive risk; business is structurally beneficiary of AI capex (more chips → more photomasks). Sympathy-fade read is clean, but basket rule enforces exploratory tier to avoid concentrating 60%+ on a single thesis reading across 4 names. 5-day move -10.84% is extended; exploratory sizing lets thesis develop without overcommit.

red_team.weakest_link: The structural beneficiary thesis (more chips → more masks) is sound, but volume_multiple of 0.59 (below average) combined with a -10.84% 5-day decline suggests conviction sellers are underweighting the AI-capex tailwind the flag claims.
red_team.critique: If PLAB is truly a mechanical beneficiary of AI chip capex, the light volume on a 5-day -10.84% move is a red flag—it suggests informed sellers are not replenishing the decline. Conviction trades typically see volume spikes on directional moves; subdued volume on a large 5-day decline often presages further weakness. The thesis is logically sound (cyber-model news should not reduce mask demand), but the price action (extended decline on light volume) contradicts the confidence level (4) assigned to the trade. Exploratory tier mitigates this, but the underlying signal is weaker than framed.

entries.name: Stride, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
entries.thesis: LRN's revenue is per-pupil state funding flowing through accredited K-12 charter contracts. The legal/regulatory wrapper (state charters, ESSA compliance, accredited transcripts) is the product. Apple shipping a better Siri or Image Playground does not let a family enroll their child or obtain a state-recognized diploma. The modest negative move is a generic edtech reflex, not a filings-supported threat.
entries.what_kills: A state AG or governor citing AI in proposing virtual-school funding cuts; charter non-renewals tied to AI-tutor alternatives — neither in filings or news today.
entries.reasoning: Apple WWDC catalyst (not basket-rule driven; only 2 Apple flags in this batch, so basket rule does not apply). LRN passes BUY eligibility: threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified', confidence=4. Filings show revenue is per-pupil state funding through accredited K-12 charter contracts; Siri/Image Playground/Shortcuts do not disrupt the credentialing/funding moat. 1-day move -3.28%, 5-day move flat (+0.24%), suggests market is already pricing normalization post-WWDC. Exploratory tier (6%) is appropriate for a macro-sympathy play with clean filings support but modest price action.

red_team.weakest_link: The regulatory/accreditation moat is real, but the 5-day move of only +0.24% (essentially flat) combined with the 1-day -3.28% decline suggests the market has already normalized expectations and there is no sympathy-fade opportunity left to capture.
red_team.critique: LRN's filings support the thesis that state K-12 charter funding is orthogonal to Apple's AI-Siri announcement. However, the five-day price action—a near-flat +0.24%—indicates the market did not treat this as a major sympathy event to begin with. Entering an exploratory position on a name that showed minimal sympathy decline to a major Apple AI announcement means the edge (fade repricing) is already gone. The -3.28% one-day move is modest and could reflect normal noise rather than genuine thematic selling. For a sympathy-fade trade to work, the market needs to have been wrong (oversold on false thematic contagion); LRN's flat 5-day price action suggests the market never believed the contagion in the first place, which eliminates the repricing opportunity.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: Anthropic releases Fable model with strict guardrails, drawing cybersecurity researcher pushback
entries.thesis: Anthropic Fable is a cybersecurity model with no overlap with HIPAA-bound provider data warehousing and EHR-integrated analytics. However, HCAT has real idiosyncratic stress (Project Nexus 9% RIF in Q2 2026, DOS-to-Ignite migration churn) that means this move could partly reflect ongoing fundamental concerns rather than pure sympathy.
entries.what_kills: The 10-Q explicitly flags continuing DOS-Ignite churn and revenue decline — if a sell-side note tied today's move to fundamentals rather than the Fable headline, the 'sympathy fade' read is wrong.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Basket-rule trigger: Fable 5 catalyst, same cohort as OMCL/CGNX/PLAB. Passes BUY eligibility: threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified', confidence=3 (threshold). 5-day move is +20.3% (recovered significantly from 1-day -6.43%), suggesting partial repricing already underway. Caveat: Project Nexus restructuring and DOS-Ignite migration are real idiosyncratic stressors, so sympathy-fade read has some noise. Basket rule requires exploratory tier; 5-day recovery makes position riskier than pure sympathy thesis, but confidence=3 matches exploratory tier threshold.

red_team.weakest_link: The +20.3% five-day recovery directly contradicts the sympathy-fade thesis and suggests the market has already repriced away the AI-event impact, leaving no edge for a late entry.
red_team.critique: HCAT's five-day move is +20.3%, which means the stock has already recovered sharply from any Fable-related sympathy selloff. A trade entered after a +20% recovery is not a fade opportunity; it is a chase entry. The flag itself acknowledges that 'Project Nexus restructuring and DOS-Ignite migration are real idiosyncratic stressors,' which means the initial -6.43% one-day move may have been driven by company-specific risk rather than sympathy to begin with. If fundamentals (churn, revenue decline per the 10-Q) are the real driver, buying after a +20% bounce is precisely when exploratory convictions tend to fail. The confidence level assigned to this decision (3, threshold) reflects the weakness, but the entry timing after a major recovery is a specific flaw not fully articulated in the reasoning.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: Apple's long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here, with new Image Playground, Photos AI editing, and Shortcuts AI workflow builder
entries.thesis: Verra's product is municipal contracts plus physical cameras with chain-of-custody evidence. Apple's consumer AI does not generate court-admissible violation evidence or hold municipal contracts. The modest drop on the WWDC day is generic 'workflow automation' sympathy with no filings support. Caveat: the five-day move is sharply negative, suggesting the market is already pricing the Commercial Services renewal uncertainty disclosed in the 10-Q — that overhang is real and separate from any AI sympathy.
entries.what_kills: The Commercial Services contract negotiation (a known overhang in the Q) producing materially worse pricing/terms — this would justify the multi-day weakness independent of Apple.
entries.reasoning: Apple WWDC catalyst, passes BUY eligibility: threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified', confidence=3. Filings show municipal camera contracts + court-admissible evidence; Siri/Image Playground do not touch that TAM. However, 5-day move is sharply negative (-14.52%), and filings flag a real concurrent Commercial Services renewal overhang (>10% customer at risk). Sympathy-fade thesis is valid, but the tape is already extended and the idiosyncratic renewal overhang makes timing uncertain. More importantly, VRRM would be the 7th exploratory position this run (after OMCL, CGNX, PLAB, HCAT, LRN). Hard cap on exploratory positions (6 per screen) forces conversion to WATCH. Revisit after dry powder frees up or Commercial Services renewal resolves.
